name: Soka / Soka Beach / Pantai Soka (Tabanan)
gps_location: https://maps.app.goo.gl/?q=-8.5867,114.9897 (approx. -8.5867, 114.9897, Jalan Raya Denpasar-Gilimanuk, Antap, Selemadeg, Kabupaten Tabanan, Bali 82162) [uncertain]
distance_from_dps_airport_km: approx. 60-70 km (about 2 hours by car from DPS Ngurah Rai Airport; ~40 km west of Denpasar) [uncertain]
distance_from_canggu_uluwatu_min: From Canggu: ~75-100 min by scooter (about 50-60 km on the Denpasar-Gilimanuk road); From Uluwatu: ~2-2.5 hours by scooter [uncertain]
wave_type: Predominantly a long left-hand point/reef-and-cobblestone break on a swelly black-sand bay; A-frame peaks also reported on smaller days; sits between Medewi (cobblestone left point) and Balian (rivermouth) on the west coast [uncertain]
skill_level: Intermediate to Advanced (strong currents, exposed west-coast swell, remote setting, not for beginners)
wave_height_range: Works best around 3-6 ft (waist to overhead); can hold bigger SW swells but tends to get unruly and walled with size [uncertain]
bottom_type: Mixed: predominantly black sand and cobblestone/rock with some shallow reef sections at the point [uncertain]
best_season: Dry season (April-October) for cleaner offshore winds and more organised swell; works year-round given west-coast exposure but wet season brings onshore W/NW winds and runoff [uncertain]
best_tide: Mid to high tide preferred (low tide exposes cobblestones/reef and gets sucky); similar tide window to Medewi/Balian [uncertain]
best_wind: Light offshore E to SE wind (typical SE trade in dry season is offshore for west-facing breaks) [uncertain]
swell_consistency_rating: 3/5 (catches plenty of west-coast swell like Balian/Medewi but very wind- and tide-dependent and rarely groomed) [uncertain]
wave_length_meters: approx. 50-150 m on a good point set; not as long as Medewi's 300-500 m left [uncertain]
wave_speed_rating: medium (mellow to medium-paced on the point sections; can be punchy/closeout on bigger beach-break days) [uncertain]
takeoff_zone_difficulty: Moderate - shifty peaks, occasional drop on bigger sets, cobblestones/rock under foot at low tide; less critical than Bukit reef but unforgiving in size [uncertain]
secondary_peaks: A-frame peaks scatter along the long black-sand bay either side of the main point; rivermouth sandbars form seasonally; no clearly named inside/outside section [uncertain]
peak_crowd_level: 1-2/5 (one of the least crowded surfable spots in Bali; you can often surf alone or with 2-3 locals)
access: Drive west on Jalan Raya Denpasar-Gilimanuk; turn off the main road at the Soka rest area / Bulung Daya area and walk down to the black-sand beach. Car or scooter access; private car/driver recommended due to distance from south Bali [uncertain]
paddle_out_method: Beach paddle-out across the black sand; for the point/reef section paddle around the cobblestones via the channel; rivermouth sandbars give easier entry on smaller days [uncertain]
entry_fee: No formal entrance fee reported; small parking donation ~IDR 5,000-10,000 to local warung/parking attendant [uncertain]
recommended_session_time: Dawn patrol to mid-morning before the onshore W/NW seabreeze fills in; late afternoon glass-offs possible in dry season
best_time_of_day: Early morning (sunrise to ~9-10 am) before wind switches onshore
nearby_warung: Soka Indah rest area (Indonesian buffet/cafeteria popular with tour buses), a few small local warungs along Jalan Raya Denpasar-Gilimanuk selling rice plates, coffee, coconuts; very limited compared to Canggu/Bukit
alternative_spot_within_10min: Within 10-20 min drive: Balian Beach (rivermouth, more consistent) to the east; further west Pulukan and Medewi point break; Kedungu and Yeh Gangga also broadly in Tabanan area
hazards: Strong currents and rips, cobblestone/rock bottom that bruises feet, exposed open-ocean sets, remote location with no lifeguards, limited mobile signal in places, runoff/debris after heavy wet-season rain
marine_hazards: Sea urchins on the rocks, occasional jellyfish, stonefish possible on reef/sandy bottom (rare), no significant shark issue reported; treat reef cuts with fresh-water flush and antiseptic and seek clinic if deep [uncertain]
reef_cut_severity_index: Medium - mostly cobblestone/rock rather than razor coral; bruises and scrapes more common than deep coral cuts; reef boots advisable at lower tides [uncertain]
rip_current_pattern: Strong sweep along the open bay; sandbar/rivermouth rips can pull surfers outside; classic west-coast longshore current especially in bigger swells [uncertain]
lineup_etiquette: Very low-key village vibe; greet locals, do not paddle around, respect priority on the point, share waves; the few regulars are mostly Tabanan locals and West Bali surf-camp guests
localism_intensity: Low (cited 1-2/10) - rarely crowded enough for serious localism; friendly fishing-village atmosphere similar to Balian/Medewi [uncertain]
respect_protocol: Greet locals with senyum (smile) and small talk, do not litter on black sand, support local warungs/parking attendants, dress modestly when walking to/from beach (it is a quiet local area)
resident_local_legends: No single iconic local legend tied specifically to Soka; broader Tabanan/Medewi/Balian local crew (e.g. Made Winada family network in west Bali) occasionally surf here [uncertain]
religious_sensitivity: Quiet Hindu fishing/farming village; respect nearby temples and ceremonial processions on Jalan Raya Denpasar-Gilimanuk; observe Nyepi (silent day) when no surfing/movement is allowed island-wide; menstruating women traditionally asked to avoid temple grounds
medical_nearby: Nearest clinics: Puskesmas Selemadeg (basic local clinic) and Puskesmas Antap; for serious injuries Tabanan General Hospital (RSUD Tabanan) ~25 km east, or BIMC/Siloam in Denpasar ~40 km east [uncertain]
nearest_clinic_distance_km: Puskesmas Selemadeg approx. 3-5 km; RSUD Tabanan approx. 25 km; BIMC/Siloam Denpasar approx. 40-50 km [uncertain]
rescue_jet_ski_available: No dedicated jet ski rescue on standby
emergency_contact: Indonesia general emergency 112; Bali Search and Rescue (BASARNAS) 115; RSUD Tabanan +62 361 811-027 [uncertain]
nearest_surf_school: No surf school directly at Soka; closest options are Pondok Pitaya Surf & Yoga at Balian Beach (~20-30 min west) and small Medewi-based instructors; lessons typically arranged via accommodation
board_rental_availability: No on-beach board rental at Soka itself; rent in Balian (Pondok Pitaya, local shops) or Medewi before driving over; some accommodations supply boards [uncertain]
surf_photographer_available: No regular land/water photographer stationed at the lineup
photographer_recommendation: Freelance west-Bali based photographers can be hired by appointment via Instagram (Balian/Medewi-based shooters); rates typically IDR 500,000-1,500,000 per session [uncertain]
accommodation_options: Bali Beach Glamping (~4 km), Nirjhara Resort, Uluaman Eco Luxury Resort, Waka Gangga (Yeh Gangga), Soka Indah Bungalows; further afield Pondok Pitaya at Balian (~20-30 min) and Made's Homestay Medewi
webcam_url: No dedicated surf cam for Soka; check nearest Balian forecast at Surfline (https://www.surfline.com/surf-report/balian/5842041f4e65fad6a7708d41) as best proxy [uncertain]
drone_allowed: Generally allowed - very few people on the wide black-sand bay; respect privacy of locals and avoid flying over nearby Pura (temples) and ceremonial sites [uncertain]
coworking_nearby: No coworking on or near the beach; closest options are in Canggu (Tropical Nomad, Outpost, B Work) ~75-100 min east [uncertain]
avg_lesson_price: Not offered at Soka directly; nearest lesson pricing via Pondok Pitaya Balian or Medewi instructors approx. IDR 350,000-600,000 per 2-hour group lesson [uncertain]
board_rental_daily: Not available at Soka itself; in Balian/Medewi approx. IDR 75,000-150,000 per day [uncertain]
water_quality_rating: Generally good in dry season due to remoteness and very low development; can carry sediment, palm fronds and river runoff in heavy wet-season rain (Pulukan/Balian rivers nearby) [uncertain]
annual_competition_window: No regular WSL/national competition window at Soka itself; nearest event is occasional local longboard contests around Medewi/Balian [uncertain]
community_events: No regular surf community events at Soka; some monthly beach clean-ups organised by Tabanan-based NGOs and west-Bali surf camps [uncertain]
